h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’d always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that does make it feel more like choosing the right column than fixing broken speech.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner initially expects to simply identify and correct their sloppy grammar mistakes to produce acceptable Standard English for their essay.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct is still to give `p < .001` a lot of weight
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to compute the saved hours and define the rollout cost threshold that would negate the business case.

scored.recoheredEarlier: picturing one big flood doing the main work
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is sequentially answering the tutor's specific prompts about calculating erosion rates and identifying physical scars versus routes.

scored.recoheredEarlier: it sounds like a verdict before the verdict.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Now I think the correction is saying that is not really a belief about what happened, but more like a rule about who has to prove the case.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue fixating on the semantic distinction between the words 'innocent' and 'not guilty yet'.
The learner iteratively refines their explanation of the trophic cascade step-by-step with the tutor's guidance.
The learner is progressively shifting trust from visual appearance to empirical tests like the scale and drying dish.
